title: Salty Indian Chicken Curry
description: A quick, lowsodium Indian chicken curry packed with fragrant turmeric and aromatic spices, delivering a savory, tender dish in just 30 minutes.

ingredients.name: Spice blend
ingredients.amount: turmeric 1 tsp, coriander 1 tsp, cumin 1/2 tsp, garam masala 1/4 tsp

instructions: Heat oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat. Add sliced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add minced garlic and ginger, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in diced tomatoes and tomato paste, cook 2 minutes until tomatoes soften.
Add chicken pieces, browning all sides for about 5 minutes.
Sprinkle the spice blend over the chicken, stir to coat, and cook 2 minutes to toast the spices.
Pour in the low‑sodium chicken broth, bring to a simmer, reduce heat to medium‑low, cover, and cook 8–10 minutes until the chicken is tender and the liquid has thickened.
Stir in salt and adjust seasoning; if the sauce needs more thickness, simmer uncovered for another 2 minutes.
Sprinkle chopped cilantro, remove from heat, and serve hot over rice or naan.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
